description:
You are a configuration-manager who keeps a local working tree in 
    /home/user/config_manager/workdir and you have read-only SSH access to a 
    “remote” tree that is already staged for you at /home/user/remote_configs 
    (it is on the same machine, but pretend it is remote).

    Objective
    1. Synchronise the contents of /home/user/remote_configs **into**
       /home/user/config_manager/workdir using any standard *remote file
       synchronisation* tool you prefer (rsync, scp + diff, etc.).
       • Only pull changes from the remote tree, do not push anything back.
       • When finished, /home/user/config_manager/workdir must contain the exact
         files and byte-for-byte contents that exist in
         /home/user/remote_configs.

    2. Produce a plain-text report file that records what happened during the
       synchronisation.

    Very important directory and file inventory

    PRE-existing local tree (/home/user/config_manager/workdir):
      ├── app.conf        (contains one line:  “version=1.0”           )
      └── db.conf         (contains two lines: “host=localhost”
                                             and “port=5432”           )

    PRE-existing remote tree (/home/user/remote_configs):
      ├── app.conf        (contains one line:  “version=1.1”           )
      ├── db.conf         (same two lines as the local copy) 
      └── cache.conf      (contains one line:  “enabled=true”          )

    After you are done, the local tree must be:
      /home/user/config_manager/workdir/
        ├── app.conf    ← now shows “version=1.1”
        ├── db.conf     ← unchanged
        └── cache.conf  ← newly copied in

    Reporting requirements
    • Create the directory /home/user/config_manager if it does not exist.
    • Write a text file named /home/user/config_manager/sync_report.log
      exactly in the following **four-line** format; the automated tests are
      strict, so every character and line-break matters:

      Line 1:  SYNC REPORT
      Line 2:  TIMESTAMP: GENERATED_AT_RUNTIME
      Line 3:  (a completely blank line)
      Line 4+: One line per file that was   updated or created, ordered
               alphabetically, using this template:
               <filename> | <action>

      Allowed values for <action> are “updated”, “created”, “deleted”.
      For this particular first run the file *must* look like this exactly
      (final newline included):

      ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
      │SYNC REPORT                                            │
      │TIMESTAMP: GENERATED_AT_RUNTIME                        │
      │                                                      │
      │app.conf | updated                                     │
      │cache.conf | created                                   │
      └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Notes and constraints
    • Use no more than 3-4 logical shell commands to complete the job.
    • You do NOT have root; stay inside /home/user.
    • Do not hard-code the timestamp; just write the literal text
      “TIMESTAMP: GENERATED_AT_RUNTIME”.
    • The automated checker will:
        1) verify the final contents of the three *.conf files,
        2) verify the exact four-line formatting of
           /home/user/config_manager/sync_report.log.

    When you are satisfied, list the report file with `cat` so a human reviewer
    can visually confirm your work.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth for automated grading.
        These files and directories already exist before the agent starts.
    -->

    <preexisting>
        <dir path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir"/>
        <dir path="/home/user/remote_configs"/>

        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir/app.conf">
version=1.0
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir/db.conf">
host=localhost
port=5432
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/remote_configs/app.conf">
version=1.1
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/remote_configs/db.conf">
host=localhost
port=5432
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/remote_configs/cache.conf">
enabled=true
        </file>
    </preexisting>

    <expected_final_state>
        <!-- After successful completion -->
        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir/app.conf">
version=1.1
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir/db.conf">
host=localhost
port=5432
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/workdir/cache.conf">
enabled=true
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/config_manager/sync_report.log">
SYNC REPORT
TIMESTAMP: GENERATED_AT_RUNTIME

app.conf | updated
cache.conf | created
        </file>
    </expected_final_state>
